title: Getting started with your Trade-in payments and invoices
bodyHtml:
What you pay for each Trade-in order

Every time you complete a Trade-in order, the amount that will be debited from your account equals the amount you paid for the Trade-in item plus commission and logistics fees. This comes either after you select “Validate order” or after a counteroffer you have made is accepted by the customer.

Learn more about commission and logistics fees

Pro tips:

  • If your headquarters are located in France, a 20% VAT (Value-Added Tax) will apply on top of your commission and logistics fees for each order you pay.
  • For each Trade-in order you generate, you pay everything you owe Back Market for this order at the same moment you are paying customers.

 

How to access your Trade-in invoices

In your Back Office, go to “Sales” > “Money” > “Past invoices” > “Trade-in” to download any of your invoices in PDF format.

Pro tips:

  • You’ll receive a new Trade-in invoice on the first day of each month that will cover all Trade-in orders paid during the prior month.
    • For example, an invoice created on 1 April 2026, will display orders you paid between 1-31 March (inclusive).
    • An order is considered paid once it has been confirmed that the customer will be paid for the order. This usually happens a couple of days after you’ve selected “Validate order” or a couple of days after your counteroffer has been accepted.

 

How to read your Trade-in invoices

In your Trade-in invoice, you’ll find:

  • Total amount you paid to Back Market for your Trade-in orders for the month. This is “Total TTC en euros” (or GBP) and it includes:
    • Commission fees: “Commissions sur commandes déclarées payés du XXX au XXX inclus”.
    • Logistic fees: “Prestation logistique sur les colis reçus du XXX au XXX inclus”.
  • Total amount you paid to customers only for your Trade-in orders of the month. This is “Montant total payé pour les reprises, du XXX au XXX (inclus).”
    • This information is indicated on your invoice sheet but does not represent an amount Back Market invoiced you.
  • List of all Trade-in order IDs you paid during the past month. This can be found at the bottom of the invoice.
  • Invoice ID (also called bill_id) at the top: “Facture n°XXX”.


Pro tips:

  • Your Trade-in invoice is issued in French. We’re working on having it translated into English.
  • Value-Added Tax:
    • “Total HT” means amounts without tax. This is only what you have to pay if you’re not based in France.
    • If you’re based in France, you’ll see non-null VAT amounts “TVA”. They apply to commission and logistic fees and are summed up in the “TVA à 20%” row.
  • In case you failed to pay an order to a customer, the order will still show at the bottom of your invoice, and fees associated will be included in the amount invoiced. See “Chargebacks” below to understand what happens in such cases.

 

How can I see the Trade-in orders I paid in a given month?

There are two different ways to find this information:

  • If you just need the list of order IDs you paid during a month, download your Trade-in invoice for that month as indicated above and check the bottom of the invoice
  • If you need to have the details about the payments you made for each order of an invoice, we recommend you do the following. (Please bear with us as we’re working to improve this process!)
    • Go to your Trade-in Back Office > “Orders” > “All orders”.
    • Select “See more filters” and filter on all orders that were created from 3 months before the issuing date of the invoice. Select “Apply” > “Export in CSV”.
    • Open .csv file, filter on all orders, and use column “bill_id” to filter on orders taken into account in the corresponding invoice.
    • Amounts for each order here do NOT include commission and logistics fees but just the amount paid to the customer. Add those fees to each order to get to the full amount you paid during the month.

 

What if I have issues with an order to be paid?

Chargebacks

When you complete the payment of an order but the payment cannot be processed (e.g. insufficient funds, bank refused payment, etc.), Back Market pays the customer for you to maintain a good experience for them. This is what is called a “chargeback”.
As a result:

  • Debt is automatically generated for your account and can be refunded as follows:
    • Automatically: This debt is added to your next sales invoice and subtracted from the amount Back Market owes you. Your sales payout can then be negative.
    • Manually: You can pay by money transfer by using the Back Market IBAN provided on your sales invoice.
  • A fee of 7.5€ or £8 is also added to your next sales invoice for each order with a failed payment.
  • Your Trade-in service is provisionally turned offline in the country of the order.

 

Credit requests

A credit request is a way of making a goodwill gesture to a customer. A goodwill gesture means any gesture to compensate the customer. In our Trade-in program, it refers to a partial or full payment of an order to the customer that Back Market can make on your behalf.

You can track those credit requests from your Sales Back Office by going to “Money” > “Goodwill gestures”.

Learn about credit requests for Trade-in

 

How to get a refund for your orders where the counteroffer was declined?

When a counteroffer you make for an order is declined, you have to send the item back to the customer within 48 hours and post the tracking ID for the return in the order chat. You can ask for a refund of the costs to ship back the item in case you respected the process to do so.

Learn about how to get your shipping costs refunded and compensation per order
